likely_department: Highways & Minor Ports Dept. / Local Body (Corporation / Municipality / Panchayat)
what_to_prepare: Exact location or address of the problem
Photo of the pothole or road damage
How long the issue has existed
Any prior complaints filed (if applicable)
expected_resolution_type: Field inspection followed by repair work. Typical timeframe: 15–30 days for urban areas.
guidance_note: For city roads, contact your Municipal Corporation. For state highways, approach the Highways Department. The CPGRAMS portal can route grievances to the relevant state or central department.

likely_department: TWAD Board (rural) / CMWSSB (Chennai) / Municipal Water Dept. (urban)
what_to_prepare: Your address and area pin code
Type of issue: no supply, contamination, low pressure, or broken pipe
How many households are affected
When the issue started
expected_resolution_type: For pipeline breaks: 24–48 hours. For supply complaints: 3–7 days depending on infrastructure.
guidance_note: Chennai residents can call CMWSSB helpline 45674567. For other districts, contact TWAD Board's local division office. Document the issue with photos if water appears discoloured.

what_to_prepare: Location of blocked drain or sewage overflow
Photos showing the blockage or flooding
Frequency of the problem (seasonal or constant)
Impact on public health or movement
expected_resolution_type: Emergency overflow: same day to 72 hours. Structural drain issues may take 30–60 days.
guidance_note: Drainage is handled by local urban bodies. Visit or call your ward councillor's office for fastest response. If stormwater and sewage are mixing, escalate as a public health issue.

likely_department: Municipal Corporation / Town Panchayat Solid Waste Management wing
what_to_prepare: Address of uncollected waste or open dumping site
How long the issue has persisted
Is it a public space, road, or near a school or water body?
Photos of the dumping location
expected_resolution_type: Urgent clearing for waste near hospitals or schools: 24–48 hours. Regular collection complaints: 2–5 days.
guidance_note: Contact your local ward office or the Swachh Bharat Mission TN unit. For large illegal dump sites, the Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board (TNPCB) also takes complaints.

likely_department: Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) / MTC (Chennai)
what_to_prepare: Bus route number or stop name
Type of issue: no buses, overcrowding, poor schedule, or unsafe driving
Time of day when the problem occurs
How many people are affected
expected_resolution_type: Schedule or service complaints: review within 7–14 days. New route requests go through formal review and may take months.
guidance_note: Chennai residents can call MTC helpline 044-25332222. For other districts, contact your local TNSTC division. Organised community petitions often get faster attention.

likely_department: Social Welfare Dept. / Adi Dravidar Welfare / BC Welfare / Revenue Dept.
what_to_prepare: Your Aadhaar and ration card details
Scheme name you are applying for or facing issues with
Documents showing eligibility (income certificate, caste certificate, etc.)
Any previous application or reference number
expected_resolution_type: Scheme eligibility queries: 7–15 days. Payment-related issues: 15–30 days depending on the scheme.
guidance_note: Visit your nearest Citizen Service Centre (CSC) or e-Sevai centre. Many TN welfare schemes can also be accessed through the Tamil Nadu e-Governance portal. MLAs' constituency offices often help with document verification.
